code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent single sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper: template map (subject will be placed before the phrase)
    tmpl = {
        "league": "plays in the {o} league",
        "manager": "{o} is the manager of {s}",
        "position": "holds the position of {o}",
        "ground": "{s}'s ground is {o}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {o}",
        "club": "plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": "was drafted by {o}",
        "height": "is {o} meters tall",
        "youthclub": "started at youth club {o}",
        "currentclub": "currently plays for {o}",
        "weight": "weighs {o} kilograms",
        "deathPlace": "died in {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started his active years in {o}",
        "currentteam": "currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": "made his debut with {o}",
        "draftPick": "was pick number {o}",
        "draftRound": "was selected in round {o}",
        "formerTeam": "formerly played for {o}",
        "playerNumber": "has player number {o}",
        "college": "attended {o}",
        "draftYear": "was drafted in {o}",
        "coach": "{o} is the coach of {s}",
        "owner": "{o} is the owner of {s}",
        "city": "is based in {o}",
        # more natural phrasing for leader and anthem
        "leader": "{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "anthem": "{o} is the national anthem of {s}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {o}",
        "language": "has language {o}",
        "generalManager": "{o} is the general manager of {s}",
        "chairman": "{o} is the chairman of {s}",
        "season": "participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": "has currency {o}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {o}",
        "stadium": "has stadium {o}",
        "universityTeam": "is the university team of {o}",
        "mostChampions": "has most championships {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {o}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {o}",
        "country": "is in {o}",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset {o}",
        "timeZone": "has time zone {o}",
        "areaTotal": "has total area {o}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Build a list of clause strings for all triples
    clauses: list[str] = []
    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate not in tmpl:
            continue
        tpl = tmpl[t.predicate]
        # If the template expects the subject placeholder, fill it accordingly
        if "{s}" in tpl:
            phrase = tpl.format(s=t.subject, o=t.object)
        else:
            phrase = f"{t.subject} {tpl.format(o=t.object)}"
        clauses.append(phrase)

    # Remove possible duplicate clauses
    seen = set()
    unique_clauses = []
    for c in clauses:
        if c not in seen:
            seen.add(c)
            unique_clauses.append(c)

    # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not unique_clauses:
        return ""
    if len(unique_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = unique_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        # join with commas, add 'and' before the last clause
        sentence = ", ".join(unique_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + unique_clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
